objective: Define an implementation-ready planning package for an org-level Local CI Gate toolkit sourced from hldpro-governance and deployable to governed repos through thin shims, without adding implementation files in this PR.
scope_boundary: Make hldpro-governance the source-of-truth repo for reusable Local CI Gate tooling.
Keep PR #256 planning-only: docs/plans artifacts, raw/cross-review planning review, raw/execution-scopes planning scope, and OVERLORD_BACKLOG mirror only.
Define the toolkit architecture, deployment model, worker/reviewer/gate handoffs, and downstream consumer boundary.
Preserve Playwright selective testing as a downstream product-repo profile pattern, not as hldpro-governance core implementation.
Use service-runbook SSOT and graphify hook helper patterns as precedents for cross-repo ownership and safe deployment.
out_of_scope: Adding implementation files under tools/, scripts/, hooks/, workflows, package files, or product repos in PR #256.
Installing lefthook, Playwright, Node scripts, Supabase dependencies, or product-repo test runners in hldpro-governance.
Copying Local CI Gate logic into consumer repos.
Closing issue #253 before Stage 6 closeout evidence exists.
Merging consumer rollout into this planning PR.
research_summary: Subagent review found the initial planning package was syntactically valid but split between planning intake and implementation planning. The plan is now explicit: hldpro-governance owns the org-level toolkit; consumer repos receive thin shims through later issue-backed rollout slices. The service runbook establishes the SSOT precedent for cross-repo runbooks, and the graphify hook helper establishes the managed-deployer precedent: resolve from governance, preflight safety before writes, and refuse unmanaged overwrite by default. The runbook also resolves prior technical ambiguities in the downstream Playwright profile by replacing xargs passthrough with a wrapper contract, defining --explain-trigger semantics, and specifying @covers * behavior.

sprints.name: Planning Package Alignment
sprints.goal: Make PR #256 coherent as the planning-only package for an org-level Local CI Gate toolkit.
tasks: Create the governing GitHub issue before adding the backlog row.
Import the runbook and reviewer-response memo under docs/plans.
Reframe the runbook around hldpro-governance as toolkit source-of-truth.
Record service-runbook SSOT and graphify-helper deployment precedents.
Add subagent planning review findings and alternate-model review evidence.
Keep all implementation files out of PR #256.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #253 is open and referenced by OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md.
The runbook, PDCA/R, structured plan, backlog row, and PR body all describe the same planning-only org-level toolkit scope.
The runbook states that consumer repos receive thin shims and that consumer rollout is separately issue-backed.
The runbook marks Playwright selective testing as a downstream product-repo profile pattern, not hldpro-governance core implementation.
Backlog alignment, structured-plan validation, diff hygiene, and PR checks pass.

sprints.name: Future Implementation Handoff
sprints.goal: Define the next implementation slice without executing it in PR #256.
tasks: Create an implementation execution-scope artifact before touching tools or scripts.
Assign a pinned Tier 2 worker for toolkit implementation.
Implement canonical toolkit source under tools/local-ci-gate/ with profile-driven checks.
Add a deployer that installs thin consumer shims from a pinned governance checkout/ref.
Dogfood the governance profile locally before consumer rollout.
Open separate issue-backed consumer rollout slices after governance dogfood passes.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation changes occur in a separate PR with accepted execution-scope evidence.
The toolkit has one canonical source in hldpro-governance.
The deployer refuses unmanaged overwrites and unsafe paths by default.
At least one governance-repo profile dry run proves the runner reports blocker/advisory/subset/full-scope behavior without claiming CI parity.
Consumer rollout issues exist before any downstream repo shim is installed.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing reusable workflow, .governance checkout, service-runbook SSOT, graphify helper, and deployment patterns.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended making hldpro-governance the source-of-truth toolkit repo with a governance-owned runner, repo-family profiles, a deployer that installs thin consumer shims, and reusable workflow/deployer patterns rather than duplicated per-repo logic.

specialist_reviews.focus: Ensure PR #256 has one authoritative story and that worker/reviewer/gate handoffs are defined before implementation.
specialist_reviews.summary: Found the package valid but split-brained: the runbook had moved toward org-level toolkit implementation while the PDCA/R and structured plan still described planning intake. Recommended aligning every artifact around planning-only org-level toolkit scope and deferring implementation to a pinned worker handoff.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code CLI
alternate_model_review.summary: Claude returned APPROVED_WITH_CHANGES with no blocking findings. Required follow-ups were applied: §1.1 now includes governance-profile implementation micro-slices G1-G5 and an explicit Local CI Gate deployer safety contract.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Planning-only org-level Local CI Gate toolkit package for issue #253: runbook, memo, PDCA/R, structured plan, cross-review, planning execution-scope artifact, and issue-backed OVERLORD_BACKLOG row. No implementation files in PR #256.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge planning package after review. Implementation begins only after a pinned Tier 2 worker execution-scope artifact exists.
blocked_on: pinned Tier 2 worker execution-scope artifact for implementation PR

material_deviation_rules: If implementation files become part of PR #256, remove them and create a separate issue-backed implementation branch.
If consumer rollout is requested, open separate issue-backed adoption slices rather than adding downstream repo changes to this PR.
If alternate-model review rejects the plan, update the planning artifacts before implementation begins.
If the toolkit architecture diverges from the service-runbook SSOT or graphify-helper managed-deployer precedent, record the reason in PDCA/R and cross-review.
Do not alter the dirty shared main checkout while this isolated worktree remains the active workspace.
